2605.12477.title: MEME: Multi-entity & Evolving Memory Evaluation
2605.12477.paper_summary: LLM-based agents increasingly operate in persistent environments where they must store, update, and reason over information across many sessions. While prior benchmarks evaluate only single-entity updates, MEME defines six tasks spanning the full space defined by the multi-entity and evolving axes, including three not scored by prior work: Cascade and Absence (dependency reasoning) and Deletion (post-removal state). Evaluating six memory systems spanning three memory paradigms on 100 controlled episodes, we find that all systems collapse on dependency reasoning under the default configuration (Cascade: 3%, Absence: 1% in average accuracy) despite adequate static retrieval performance. Prompt optimization, deeper retrieval, reduced filler noise, and most stronger LLMs fail to close this gap. Only a file-based agent paired with Claude Opus 4.7 as its internal LLM partially closes the gap, but at ~70x the baseline cost, indicating closure currently depends on configurations that are not practical at scale. Code and data are available on the project page: https://seokwonjung-jay.github.io/meme-eval/.
2605.12477.paper_summary_zh: LLM 基礎的代理人越來越多地在持久環境中運作，必須在多個會話中儲存、更新和推理信息。雖然先前的基準僅評估單一實體的更新，但 MEME 定義了六個任務，涵蓋了多實體和演變軸所定義的完整空間，包括三個先前工作未評分的任務：Cascade 和 Absence（依賴推理）以及 Deletion（移除後狀態）。在 100 個控制的實驗中評估六個記憶系統，涵蓋三種記憶範式，我們發現所有系統在默認配置下的依賴推理表現不佳（Cascade: 平均準確率 3%，Absence: 平均準確率 1%），儘管靜態檢索性能足夠。提示優化、更深的檢索、減少填充噪音以及大多數更強的 LLM 都未能縮小這一差距。只有一個與 Claude Opus 4.7 配對的基於文件的代理人部分縮小了這一差距，但成本約為基線的 70 倍，這表明目前的縮小依賴於不適合大規模的配置。代碼和數據可在項目頁面上獲得：https://seokwonjung-jay.github.io/meme-eval/.
2605.12477.author: Seokwon Jung et.al.
2605.12477.authors: Seokwon Jung, Alexander Rubinstein, Arnas Uselis, Sangdoo Yun, Seong Joon Oh
